title: 重命名扩展元数据与配置命名空间
stageGoal: 更新 package.json 中的扩展名称、展示名称、描述、激活入口、聊天参与者 id/name、配置标题和 configuration key 前缀，将 ralph-runner 命名空间完整替换为 harness-runner，并确保 VS Code 贡献点与运行时代码一致。
summary: Completed the harness-runner namespace rename for package metadata, command ids, chat participant ids/names, configuration keys, runtime registrations, related localization bindings, tests, and README usage examples.
architectureNotes: This story was kept inside the contribution-point boundary: package.json, extension command registration, chat participant wiring, localization command links, tests, and README guidance.
The stable runtime persistence boundary remains .harness-runner, so the rename does not require storage-path migrations or artifact-move logic.

The safest split point for future follow-up work is packaging/distribution branding versus broader user-visible runtime copy, because those concerns can evolve without re-breaking command registration.
keyDecisions: Rename package and runtime command ids together so VS Code manifest registration stays exact.
Rename the chat participant and slash command to @harness /harness-spec and align the prompt-builder wording in src/projectConstraints.ts.
Write new workspace-pinned reviewer settings under harness-runner.* and delete old ralph-runner workspace keys when persisting overrides.
confirmedConstraints: No dist/ or out/ files were hand-edited.
Validation ran through npm run compile, npm run compile-tests, and npm test.
Git commit metadata must use Chinese.
unresolvedRisks: package.json repository.url and the checked-in VSIX filename still use the legacy ralph-runner slug.
Broader RALPH-branded runtime/help copy still exists outside this focused namespace rename surface.
Project-constraints and agent-map artifacts may need regeneration after the rebrand settles.
nextStoryPrerequisites: Reviewer should verify package.json contribution ids against src/extension.ts registrations and src/localization.ts menu bindings.
Reviewer should decide whether packaging/distribution artifacts should be regenerated as part of the next pass.
If broader brand-copy cleanup continues, treat it as a separate story from command/config namespace stability.
resumeRecommendation: Reviewer can continue from this completed state by checking the renamed contribution-point cluster, then deciding whether to queue a separate packaging/doc-refresh story for remaining legacy branding surfaces.
